questions: Describe in detail how is natural selection can, over long evolutionary timescales, build increasingly more and more complex structures, functions, and adaptive behaviors from small inherited differences among individuals, as those differences affect survival, mating success, competition, cooperation, and resilience under stress, allowing useful variations to spread, accumulate, and interact across generations in species occupying many different ecological niches and environmental conditions. Also explain how intermediate stages can remain useful, how trade-offs can shape outcomes, and how previously evolved features may be modified for new functions rather than being created from nothing in a single step.
